narrative.headline: What 2018 Honda Accord owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine problems are the most commonly reported issue, with owners describing dashboard warning lights, module failures, and excessive road noise. Forward collision avoidance complaints center on the brake assist and lane assist systems engaging unexpectedly while driving. Electrical faults and fuel system issues round out the picture, with some complaints in each area associated with fires or crashes.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report dashboard warning lights illuminating within days of purchase, module replacements, and pronounced road and engine noise. · 3 fire · 6 crash · 1 injured

issues.sample: TL* THE CONTACT OWNS A 2018 HONDA ACCORD. WHILE DRIVING VARIOUS SPEEDS, THE ROAD TERRAIN CHANGED AND THE BRAKE ASSIST ENGAGED, WHICH CAUSED THE VEHICLE TO STOP ABRUPTLY. IN ADDITION, THE LANE  ASSIST FEATURE WAS PULLING THE VEHICLE. THE BRAKE ASSIST AND LANE ASSIST WARNING INDICA
issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report the brake assist engaging abruptly and without clear cause when road terrain changes, and the lane assist system pulling the vehicle unexpectedly. · 6 crash · 11 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report the fuel filler door freezing and becoming impossible to open, with dealers acknowledging the issue but offering no repair timeline. · 4 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report all dashboard warning indicators illuminating simultaneously while the vehicle is in motion. · 2 fire · 11 crash · 6 injured
